name: svcs
documentation:
A Flexible Service Locator for Python.

*svcs* (pronounced *services*) is a **dependency container** for Python. It gives you a central place to register factories for types/interfaces and then imperatively acquire instances of those types with **automatic cleanup** and **health checks**. It's suitable for implementing [Inversion of Control](https://svcs.hynek.me/en/latest/glossary.html#term-Inversion-of-Control) using either **dependency injection** or **service location** while not requiring global state, decorators, or mangling of function signatures. Benefits: - Eliminates tons of repetitive **boilerplate** code, - unifies **acquisition** and **cleanups** of services, - provides full *static* **type safety** for them, - simplifies **testing** through **loose coupling**, - improves *live* **introspection** and **monitoring** with **health checks**. The goal is to minimize the code for acquiring pluggable services to: ```python from svcs.your_framework import svcs_from def view(request): db, api, cache = svcs_from(request).get(Database, WebAPIClient, Cache) ``` ... or less! To a type checker like [Mypy](https://mypy-lang.org), `db` has the type `Database`, `api` has the type `WebAPIClient`, and `cache` has the type `Cache`. `db`, `api`, and `cache` will be automatically cleaned up when the request ends -- it's context managers all the way down. *svcs* comes with seamless integration for **AIOHTTP**, **FastAPI**, **Flask**, **Pyramid**, and **Starlette**. While *svcs* also has first-class support for static typing, it is **strictly optional** and will always remain so. *svcs* also doesn't check your types at runtime. It only forwards the type you have asked for to the type checker. If you don't use a type checker, that information is ignored without any runtime overhead. Read on in [*Why?*](https://svcs.hynek.me/en/latest/why.html) or watch this short video if you're intrigued: [![Watch the video](https://img.youtube.com/vi/d1elMD9WgpA/maxresdefault.jpg)](https://youtu.be/d1elMD9WgpA) ## Project Links - [**PyPI**](https://pypi.org/project/svcs/) - [**GitHub**](https://github.com/hynek/svcs) - [**Documentation**](https://svcs.hynek.me) - [**Changelog**](https://github.com/hynek/svcs/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) - [**Funding**](https://hynek.me/say-thanks/) ## Release Information ### Fixed - AIOHTTP: The registry is now stored using `aiohttp.web.AppKey`s on the application. This is an implementation detail and shouldn't matter, but it fixes a warning on AIOHTTP 3.9 and later. --- [Full Changelog →](https://github.com/hynek/svcs/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) ## Credits *svcs* is written by [Hynek Schlawack](https://hynek.me/) and distributed under the terms of the [MIT](https://github.com/hynek/svcs/blob/main/LICENSE) license. The development is kindly supported by my employer [Variomedia AG](https://www.variomedia.de/) and all my fabulous [GitHub Sponsors](https://github.com/sponsors/hynek). The [Bestagon](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=thOifuHs6eY) radar logo is made by [Lynn Root](https://www.roguelynn.com), based on an [Font Awesome](https://fontawesome.com) icon. *svcs* has started out as a wrapper around [*wired*](https://wired.readthedocs.io/) by [Michael Merickel](https://michael.merickel.org/) and has been heavily influenced by it.

modules.name: svcs.fastapi
documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2023 Hynek Schlawack <hs@ox.cx>
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: svcs.fastapi.container
documentation:
A FastAPI [dependency](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/dependencies/) that provides you
with a request-scoped container.

Yields:

: A {class}`svcs.Container` that is cleaned up after the request.

variables.name: svcs.fastapi.DepContainer
documentation:
An alias for:

```
typing.Annotated[svcs.Container, fastapi.Depends(svcs.fastapi.container)]
```

This allows you write your view like:

```
@app.get("/")
async def view(services: svcs.fastapi.DepContainer):
    ...
```

classes.name: svcs.fastapi.lifespan
documentation:
Make a FastAPI lifespan *svcs*-aware.

Makes sure that the registry is available to the decorated lifespan
function as a second parameter and that the registry is closed when the
application exists.

Async generators are automatically wrapped into an async context manager.

Args:
    lifespan: The lifespan function to make *svcs*-aware.

modules.name: svcs.exceptions
documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2023 Hynek Schlawack <hs@ox.cx>
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

classes.name: svcs.exceptions.ServiceNotFoundError
documentation: Raised when the requested service type is not registered.

modules.name: svcs.starlette
documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2023 Hynek Schlawack <hs@ox.cx>
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: svcs.starlette.svcs_from
documentation: Get the current container from *request*.

functions.name: svcs.starlette.get_pings
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Container.get_pings`, but uses the container from
*request*.

See Also:

: {ref}`aiohttp-health`

functions.name: svcs.starlette.aget_abstract
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Container.aget_abstract()`, but uses container from
*request*.

functions.name: svcs.starlette.aget
documentation: Same as {meth}`svcs.Container.aget`, but uses the container from *request*.

classes.name: svcs.starlette.lifespan
documentation:
Make a Starlette lifespan *svcs*-aware.

Makes sure that the registry is available to the decorated lifespan
function as a second parameter and that the registry is closed when the
application exists.

Async generators are automatically wrapped into an async context manager.

Args:
    lifespan: The lifespan function to make *svcs*-aware.

classes.name: svcs.starlette.SVCSMiddleware
documentation:
Attach a {class}`svcs.Container` to the request state, based on a registry
that has been put on the request state by {class}`lifespan`. Closes the
container at the end of a request or websocket connection.

modules.name: svcs
documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2023 Hynek Schlawack <hs@ox.cx>
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

modules.name: svcs.flask
documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2023 Hynek Schlawack <hs@ox.cx>
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: svcs.flask.svcs_from
documentation: Get the current container from *g*.

functions.name: svcs.flask.get_registry
documentation:
Get the registry from *app* or {obj}`flask.current_app`.

Args:

: app: If None, {obj}`flask.current_app` is used.

:::{versionadded} 23.21.0 *app* can be None, in which case :obj:`flask.current_app` is used.
:::

functions.name: svcs.flask.init_app
documentation:
Initialize *app* for *svcs*.

Creates a registry for you if you don't provide one.

functions.name: svcs.flask.get_abstract
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Container.get_abstract()`, but uses container on
{obj}`flask.g`.

functions.name: svcs.flask.register_factory
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Registry.register_factory()`, but uses registry on
*app* that has been put there by {func}`init_app()`.

functions.name: svcs.flask.register_value
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Registry.register_value()`, but uses registry on *app*
that has been put there by {func}`init_app()`.

functions.name: svcs.flask.overwrite_factory
documentation:
Obtain the currently active container on `g` and overwrite the factory
for *svc_type*.

Afterwards resets the instantiation cache on `g`.

See Also:
: - {meth}`svcs.Registry.register_factory()`
  - {meth}`svcs.Container.close()`

functions.name: svcs.flask.overwrite_value
documentation:
Obtain the currently active container on `g` and overwrite the value
for *svc_type*.

Afterwards resets the instantiation cache on `g`.

See Also:
: - {meth}`svcs.Registry.register_factory()`
  - {meth}`svcs.Container.close()`

functions.name: svcs.flask.get_pings
documentation:
See {meth}`svcs.Container.get_pings()`.

See Also:

: {ref}`flask-health`

functions.name: svcs.flask.teardown
documentation:
To be used with {meth}`flask.Flask.teardown_appcontext` that requires to
take an exception.

The app context is torn down after the response is sent.

functions.name: svcs.flask.close_registry
documentation: Close the registry on *app*, if present.

functions.name: svcs.flask.get
documentation: Same as {meth}`svcs.Container.get()`, but uses container on {obj}`flask.g`.

modules.name: svcs.pyramid
documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2023 Hynek Schlawack <hs@ox.cx>
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: svcs.pyramid.svcs_from
documentation:
Get the current container either from *request* or from thread locals.

Args:
    request: If None, thread locals are used.

functions.name: svcs.pyramid.get_registry
documentation:
Get the registry from *rh* or thread locals.

Args:
    rh: If None, thread locals are used.

functions.name: svcs.pyramid.init
documentation:
Configure *config* to work with *svcs*.

*svcs* uses a {term}`tween` to manage the life cycle of the container. You
can affect its position by passing *tween_under* and *tween_over*.

Args:

: config: Pyramid configurator object.

  registry:

  : A custom *svcs* registry to use. If None, a new one is created.

  tween_under:

  : Passed unchanged to {meth}`pyramid.config.Configurator.add_tween()`
    as *under*.

  tween_over:

  : Passed unchanged to {meth}`pyramid.config.Configurator.add_tween()`
    as *over*.

[tween]: https://docs.pylonsproject.org/projects/pyramid/en/main/glossary.html#term-tween

functions.name: svcs.pyramid.register_factory
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Registry.register_factory()`, but uses registry on
*config*.

functions.name: svcs.pyramid.register_value
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Registry.register_value()`, but uses registry on
*config*.

functions.name: svcs.pyramid.close_registry
documentation:
Close the registry on *rh*, if present.

Ideal for {func}`atexit.register()` handlers.

Args:

: rh: An object that carries a {class}`pyramid.registry.Registry`.

functions.name: svcs.pyramid.get_pings
documentation:
Like {meth}`svcs.Container.get_pings()`, but uses container on *request*.

See Also:

: {ref}`pyramid-health`

functions.name: svcs.pyramid.get_abstract
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Container.get_abstract()`, but uses container from
*request*.

functions.name: svcs.pyramid.get
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Container.get()`, but uses thread locals to find the
current request.

classes.name: svcs.pyramid.ServicesTween
documentation: Handle *svcs* container life cycle for a Pyramid request.

classes.name: svcs.pyramid.PyramidRegistryHaver
documentation:
An object with a {class}`pyramid.registry.Registry` as a `registry`
attribute. For example a {class}`~pyramid.config.Configurator` or an
application.

modules.name: svcs.aiohttp
documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2023 Hynek Schlawack <hs@ox.cx>
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: svcs.aiohttp.svcs_from
documentation: Get the current container from *request*.

functions.name: svcs.aiohttp.get_registry
documentation: Get the registry from *app*.

functions.name: svcs.aiohttp.init_app
documentation:
Initialize the application.

Inserts the *svcs* middleware at *middleware_pos* which is 0 by default, so
you can use {func}`svcs_from` and {func}`aget` in other middlewares.

functions.name: svcs.aiohttp.register_value
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Registry.register_value()`, but uses registry on
*app*.

functions.name: svcs.aiohttp.register_factory
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Registry.register_factory()`, but uses registry on
*app*.

functions.name: svcs.aiohttp.aclose_registry
documentation:
Close the registry on *app*, if present.

You probably don't have to call this yourself, because it's registered for
the application as an \{attr}\`aiohttp.web.Application.on_cleanup\` callback.

See Also:

: {ref}`aiohttp-cleanup`

functions.name: svcs.aiohttp.get_pings
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Container.get_pings`, but uses the container from
*request*.

See Also:

: {ref}`aiohttp-health`

functions.name: svcs.aiohttp.aget_abstract
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Container.aget_abstract()`, but uses container from
*request*.

functions.name: svcs.aiohttp.aget
documentation: Same as {meth}`svcs.Container.aget`, but uses the container from *request*.

variables.name: svcs.aiohttp._AIOHTTP_KEY_REGISTRY
documentation: type: ignore[assignment]

modules.name: svcs._core
documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2023 Hynek Schlawack <hs@ox.cx>
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: svcs._core._takes_container
documentation: Return True if *factory* takes a svcs.Container as its first argument.

classes.name: svcs._core.ServicePing

methods.name: svcs._core.ServicePing.ping
documentation:
Instantiate the service, schedule its cleanup, and call its ping
method.

methods.name: svcs._core.ServicePing.aping
documentation:
Same as {meth}`ping` but instantiate and/or ping asynchronously, if
necessary.

Also works with synchronous services, so in an async application, just
use this.

A service health check as returned by {meth}`svcs.Container.get_pings`.

Attributes:

: name: A fully-qualified name of the service type.

  is_async: Whether the service needs to be pinged using {meth}`aping`.

See Also:

: {ref}`health`

classes.name: svcs._core.Registry

methods.name: svcs._core.Registry.__contains__
documentation:
Check whether this registry knows how to create *svc_type*:

```
.. doctest::

    >>> reg = svcs.Registry()
    >>> reg.register_value(int, 42)
    >>> int in reg
    True
    >>> str in reg
    False
```

methods.name: svcs._core.Registry.__del__
documentation: Warn if the registry is gc'ed before being closed.

methods.name: svcs._core.Registry.register_factory
documentation:
Register *factory* to be used when asked for a *svc_type*.

Repeated registrations overwrite previous ones, but the
*on_registry_close* callbacks are run all together when the registry is
closed.

Args:

: svc_type: The type of the service to register.

  factory:

  : A callable that is used to instantiated *svc_type* if asked. If
    it's a generator or a context manager, a cleanup is registered
    after instantiation.

    Can also be an async callable/generator/context manager.

    If *factory* takes a first argument called `svcs_container`
    or the first argument (of any name) is annotated as being
    {class}`svcs.Container`, the container instance that is
    instantiating the service is passed into the factory as the
    first positional argument.

    Note:

    : Generally speaking, given the churn and edgecases in the
      typing ecosystem, we recommend using the name route to
      detect the container argument because it's most reliable.

  enter:

  : Whether to enter context managers if one is returned by
    *factory*. Usually you want that, but there are occasions --
    like database transaction managers -- that you want to enter
    manually.

  ping:

  : A callable that marks the service as having a health check.

    See Also:

    : {meth}`Container.get_pings` and {class}`ServicePing`.

  on_registry_close:

  : A callable that is called when the
    {meth}`svcs.Registry.close()` method is called.

    Can also be an async callable or an
    {class}`collections.abc.Awaitable`; then
    {meth}`svcs.Registry.aclose()` must be called.

methods.name: svcs._core.Registry.register_value
documentation:
Syntactic sugar for:

```
register_factory(
    svc_type,
    lambda: value,
    enter=enter,
    ping=ping,
    on_registry_close=on_registry_close
)
```

Please note that, unlike with {meth}`register_factory`, entering
context managers is **disabled** by default.

:::{versionchanged} 23.21.0 *enter* is now ``False`` by default.
:::

methods.name: svcs._core.Registry.close
documentation:
Clear registrations and run synchronous *on_registry_close* callbacks.

Async callbacks are *not* awaited and a warning is raised

Errors are logged at warning level, but otherwise ignored.

methods.name: svcs._core.Registry.aclose
documentation:
Clear registrations and run all *on_registry_close* callbacks.

Errors are logged at warning level, but otherwise ignored.

Also works with synchronous services, so in an async application, just
use this.

A central registry of recipes for creating services.

An instance of this should live as long as your application does.

Also works as a context manager that runs `on_registry_close` callbacks
on exit:

```
.. doctest::

    >>> import svcs
    >>> with svcs.Registry() as reg:
    ...     reg.register_value(
    ...         int, 42,
    ...         on_registry_close=lambda: print("closed!")
    ...     )
    closed!
```

`async with` is also supported.

Warns:
: ResourceWarning:

  : If a registry with pending cleanups is garbage-collected.

classes.name: svcs._core.Container

methods.name: svcs._core.Container.__contains__
documentation: Check whether this container has a cached instance of *svc_type*.

methods.name: svcs._core.Container.__del__
documentation: Warn if the container is gc'ed before being closed.

methods.name: svcs._core.Container.close
documentation:
Run all registered *synchronous* cleanups.

Async closes are *not* awaited and a warning is raised.

Errors are logged at warning level, but otherwise ignored.

Hint:
    The Container can be used again after this. Closing it is an
    idempotent way to reset it.

methods.name: svcs._core.Container.aclose
documentation:
Run *all* registered cleanups -- synchronous **and** asynchronous.

Errors are logged at warning level, but otherwise ignored.

Also works with synchronous services, so in an async application, just
use this.

Hint:
    The container can be used again after this. Closing it is an
    idempotent way to reset it.

methods.name: svcs._core.Container.get_pings
documentation:
Return all services that have defined a *ping* and bind them to this
container.

Returns:
    A list of services that have registered a ping callable.

methods.name: svcs._core.Container.get_abstract
documentation:
Like {meth}`get` but is annotated to return {data}`typing.Any` which
allows it to be used with abstract types like {class}`typing.Protocol`
or {mod}`abc` classes.

Note:

: See {doc}`typing-caveats` why this is necessary.

methods.name: svcs._core.Container.aget_abstract
documentation:
Same as {meth}`get_abstract` but instantiates asynchronously, if
necessary.

Also works with synchronous services, so in an async application, just
use this.

methods.name: svcs._core.Container._lookup
documentation:
Look up svc_type first in our cache, then in the registry.

If it's cached, only the first two items of the returned tupled are
meaningful.

methods.name: svcs._core.Container.register_local_factory
documentation:
Same as {meth}`svcs.Registry.register_factory()`, but registers the
factory only for this container.

A temporary {class}`svcs.Registry` is transparently created -- and
closed together with the container it belongs to.

See Also:

: {ref}`local-registries`

:::{versionadded} 23.21.0
:::

methods.name: svcs._core.Container.register_local_value
documentation:
Syntactic sugar for:

```
register_local_factory(
    svc_type,
    lambda: value,
    enter=enter,
    ping=ping,
    on_registry_close=on_registry_close
)
```

Please note that, unlike with {meth}`register_local_factory`, entering
context managers is **disabled** by default.

See Also:

: {ref}`local-registries`

:::{versionadded} 23.21.0
:::

methods.name: svcs._core.Container.get
documentation:
Get services of *svc_types*.

Instantiate them if necessary and register their cleanup.

Returns:

: `svc_types[0]` | `tuple[*svc_types]`: If one service is
  requested, it's returned directly. If multiple are requested, a
  tuple of services is returned.

methods.name: svcs._core.Container.aget
documentation:
Same as {meth}`get` but instantiates asynchronously, if necessary.

Also works with synchronous services, so in an async application, just
use this.

A per-context container for instantiated services and cleanups.

The instance of this should live as long as a request or a task.

Also works as a context manager that runs clean ups on exit:

```
.. doctest::

    >>> reg = svcs.Registry()
    >>> def factory() -> str:
    ...     yield "Hello World"
    ...     print("Cleaned up!")
    >>> reg.register_factory(str, factory)

    >>> with svcs.Container(reg) as con:
    ...     _ = con.get(str)
    Cleaned up!
```

Warns:
: ResourceWarning:

  : If a container with pending cleanups is garbage-collected.

Attributes:
: registry:

  : The {class}`Registry` instance that this container uses for service
    type lookup.
